structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan OFF and airflow reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Airflow sensor calibration alarm condition from 5.9.6.4.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Damper Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If damper position is 0% and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Damper at 0% and airflow > max(10% Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes with fan ON
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send requests for cooling SAT reset based on zone temperature exceeding cooling setpoint or cooling loop output. If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. If by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. If cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature or cooling loop conditions
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 3, 2, 1, or 0 requests as per conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
judge_reason: Cooling SAT reset request logic from 5.9.8.1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low-Discharge Air Temperature Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If heating hot-water plant is proven ON and DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 4 alarm. If DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress if zone Importance-Multiplier is 0 for hot-water reset T&R loop.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating plant ON and DAT below setpoint by thresholds for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm for 8.3°C below, Level 3 alarm for 17°C below. Suppress if Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its hot-water reset T&R control loop, low-DAT alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: a. If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its hot-water reset T&R control loop, low-DAT alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: a. If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its hot-water reset T&R control loop, low-DAT alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
judge_reason: Low discharge air temperature alarm with plant status and suppression from 5.9.6.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If less than 50% for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress if zone Importance-Multiplier is 0 for static pressure reset T&R loop.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 70% or < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes with setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm for <70%, Level 3 alarm for <50%. Suppress if Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
judge_reason: Low airflow alarm thresholds and suppression from 5.9.6.1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Alarm (Status Mismatch)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm is indicated by status input being different from output command after 15 seconds after a change in output status. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds after command change
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm if commanded ON but status OFF; Level 4 if commanded OFF but status ON.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
evidence_text: Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status. a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2 b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
judge_reason: Fan alarm status mismatch rule with delay and alarm levels from 5.9.6.3.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband mode, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint and the heating coil is OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set active primary airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint. b. Heating coil is OFF.

evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint. b. Heating coil is OFF.
evidence_text: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint. b. Heating coil is OFF.
judge_reason: Direct operational rule from 5.9.5.2 with clear setpoint and coil state.

structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If valve position is 0% for 15 minutes and DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Valve at 0% for 15 minutes and DAT > AHU SAT by 3°C
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, and DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_text: If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, and DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Damper Modulation to Maintain Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate VAV damper to maintain measured airflow at active primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.

evidence_quote: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
evidence_text: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Control Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating or Cooling, or Zone Group is Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Run fan. Before start, drive damper fully closed. After fan proven ON for 15 seconds, release damper override.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.

evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.
evidence_text: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.
judge_reason: Specific fan control sequence with damper interlock and startup delay from 5.9.5.5.
